raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Improving the draft centrally includes sharpening the thesis, motivation, and contribution framing at the manuscript level.","R1.2":"The draft makes causal and theoretical claims whose argument chain would be expected to be checked during improvement.","R1.3":"The reported empirical gains and ablations are central to the draft, so improvement should ensure claims track the stated evidence.","R1.5":"A broad request to improve the draft includes responsibility for section-level narrative order and placement of motivation, method, evidence, and prior work.","R1.6":"Writing polish is load-bearing because the delegation explicitly asks to improve a draft without a narrower technical qualifier.","RX.1":"Any improvement projection must preserve uncertainty around empirical, theoretical, and literature claims that are not independently verified.","RX.2":"The draft contains strong claims about contamination, causality, and unbiased estimation, so avoiding overclaiming is active.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within draft-improvement responsibility and not perform unrelated research, execution, or artifact transformation beyond the delegation.","RX.4":"Because the topic concerns detecting worker LLM use and reweighting human labels, harm risks around surveillance, misclassification, and worker treatment must be considered.","RX.5":"Changes or judgments should remain traceable to the provided artifact and distinguish stated evidence from inferred responsibility."}}

rationale.R1.1: Improving the draft centrally includes sharpening the thesis, motivation, and contribution framing at the manuscript level.
rationale.R1.2: The draft makes causal and theoretical claims whose argument chain would be expected to be checked during improvement.
rationale.R1.3: The reported empirical gains and ablations are central to the draft, so improvement should ensure claims track the stated evidence.
rationale.R1.5: A broad request to improve the draft includes responsibility for section-level narrative order and placement of motivation, method, evidence, and prior work.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish is load-bearing because the delegation explicitly asks to improve a draft without a narrower technical qualifier.
rationale.RX.1: Any improvement projection must preserve uncertainty around empirical, theoretical, and literature claims that are not independently verified.
rationale.RX.2: The draft contains strong claims about contamination, causality, and unbiased estimation, so avoiding overclaiming is active.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within draft-improvement responsibility and not perform unrelated research, execution, or artifact transformation beyond the delegation.
rationale.RX.4: Because the topic concerns detecting worker LLM use and reweighting human labels, harm risks around surveillance, misclassification, and worker treatment must be considered.
rationale.RX.5: Changes or judgments should remain traceable to the provided artifact and distinguish stated evidence from inferred responsibility.
